q: Which of the following is an example of a simile?
opts: The wind howled like a wounded animal
The wind was a furious beast
The wind howled angrily through the night
The wind, the furious beast, tore through the trees

q: What is the effect of using a one-sentence paragraph in a piece of creative writing?
opts: It creates emphasis and draws the reader's attention
It makes the writing feel more formal
It allows the writer to include more detail
It signals the end of a section

q: Which sentence is punctuated correctly?
opts: Having finished his homework, James went to the park.
Having finished his homework James went to the park.
Having finished his homework; James went to the park.
Having finished his homework: James went to the park.

q: In AQA GCSE English Language Paper 1, what is Question 2 about?
opts: Analysing how the writer uses language
Retrieving information from the text
Analysing the structure of the text
Writing a creative or descriptive piece

q: Which of the following is an example of pathetic fallacy?
opts: The storm clouds gathered menacingly as the argument began
The clouds were like grey blankets in the sky
The clouds drifted slowly across the horizon
The clouds, grey and heavy, hung in the air

q: What is the purpose of a colon in a sentence?
opts: To introduce a list, explanation, or quotation
To separate two independent clauses
To indicate a pause shorter than a full stop
To show possession

q: Which structural feature describes when a story starts in the middle of the action?
opts: In medias res
Foreshadowing
Cyclical structure
Flashback

q: Which of the following sentences uses the correct form of 'there', 'their', or 'they're'?
opts: They're going to collect their books from over there.
There going to collect their books from over they're.
Their going to collect there books from over they're.
They're going to collect there books from over their.

q: What is the effect of using short, staccato sentences in a piece of descriptive writing?
opts: They create tension, urgency, or a sense of panic
They make the description more detailed
They slow down the pace of the narrative
They signal a change in time or setting

q: In AQA GCSE English Language Paper 1, what is Question 5?
opts: A creative or descriptive writing task worth 40 marks
A language analysis question worth 8 marks
A structural analysis question worth 8 marks
A retrieval question worth 4 marks

q: Which language technique involves giving human qualities to non-human things?
opts: Personification
Simile
Metaphor
Onomatopoeia

q: What is the effect of a cyclical structure in a narrative?
opts: It creates a sense of resolution or inevitability by returning to the starting point
It confuses the reader by jumping between time periods
It builds suspense by delaying the ending
It allows the writer to introduce multiple characters

q: Which sentence contains a comma splice?
opts: It was raining heavily, we decided to stay indoors.
It was raining heavily, so we decided to stay indoors.
It was raining heavily. We decided to stay indoors.
Because it was raining heavily, we decided to stay indoors.

q: What is the purpose of Question 1 in AQA GCSE English Language Paper 1?
opts: To select four correct statements from a list about the source text
To analyse the writer's use of language
To analyse the structure of the text
To evaluate the text critically

q: Which of the following best describes 'show, don't tell' in creative writing?
opts: Using sensory details and actions to imply emotions rather than stating them directly
Describing every object in the room in detail
Using dialogue instead of narration
Writing in the first person throughout

q: What is the effect of using a semantic field in a text?
opts: It creates a consistent atmosphere or theme through related vocabulary
It makes the text more difficult to understand
It signals a change in the narrator's perspective
It adds humour through unexpected word choices

q: Which sentence uses the apostrophe correctly to show possession?
opts: The children's toys were scattered across the floor.
The childrens' toys were scattered across the floor.
The childrens toys were scattered across the floor.
The children's toy's were scattered across the floor.

q: What is the purpose of a shift in focus or perspective in a piece of descriptive writing?
opts: To guide the reader from a wide view to a specific detail, creating a cinematic effect
To confuse the reader intentionally
To introduce a new character
To signal the end of the description

q: Which of the following is an example of a metaphor?
opts: The classroom was a prison
The classroom was like a prison
The classroom felt as cold as a prison
The classroom reminded me of a prison

q: In the WLV English Equivalency exam, how much time is recommended for Question 5 (creative writing)?
opts: 45 minutes
20 minutes
30 minutes
60 minutes
